text: Actions are judged by intentions.
The best among you are those who have the best manners.
Make things easy and do not make them difficult.
None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
A Muslim is one from whose tongue and hand others are safe.
The strong person is not the one who can wrestle, but the one who controls himself when angry.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should speak good or remain silent.
Cleanliness is half of faith.
The best of people are those who are most beneficial to others.
Allah does not look at your appearance or wealth, but at your hearts and deeds.
He who does not show mercy will not be shown mercy.
Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.
The best charity is that given in Ramadan.
A good word is charity.
Smiling in the face of your brother is charity.
Do not envy one another.
The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small.
Whoever removes a hardship of a believer, Allah will remove one of his hardships on the Day of Judgment.
The upper hand is better than the lower hand.
Feed the hungry and visit the sick.
Exchange gifts, as that will lead to increasing your love for one another.
Do not become angry.
Part of the perfection of one's Islam is leaving what does not concern him.
The believer is not one who eats his fill while his neighbor is hungry.
Whoever cheats is not one of us.
Modesty is part of faith.
The best among you are those who learn the Qur’an and teach it.
Religion is sincerity.
The supplication is worship.
Beware of suspicion, for suspicion is the worst of false tales.
